rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees, Final Distribution

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Court Order To Distribute To Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) It is unclear how this petition belongs in the probate court in this county. The trustee resides out of state, the subject of this petition is proceeds from sale of real property in Texas, no determination is being requested to be made regarding the trust, and Petitioner brings this petition under Probate Code 11700, which is used to determine those entitled to distribution in decedent's estate proceedings (this is a trust matter, it is already known who is entitled to distribution, and Petitioner is requesting determination of who is entitled to proceeds of real property, not trust assets). 2) 30-day Notice of Hearing on filing amended petition has not been provided. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE COTTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/19/2021. 1) No DE-120 form is on file. 2) A supplement verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Has any creditor claim filed or presented? B) Was notice to any public entity under Probate Code section 9201 required or given? C) The exact date of mailing notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be specified. D) What is the current combined balance of the two estate bank accounts? E) The amount distributable to each beneficiary after payment of the taxes authorized by the Court on 03/19/2021 must be specified. F) The reserve amount must be adjusted based on the tax payments already made from the blocked account. 3) The inventory and appraisal filed on 04/02/2019 did not specify the decedent's ownership percentage. A corrected inventory and appraisal showing 67% value of the real property as of 11/30/2012 must be filed. A proof of service showing that a copy of the corrected inventory and appraisal was given to Aurora Ingram who requested special notice must also be filed. 4) The petitioner's statutory compensation was incorrectly calculated. Also, the compensation must be calculated based on the decedent's share of the sale price. If the petitioner desires to increase the compensation, an amendment to the petition must be filed, and notice of the amendment must be given to the beneficiaries on a DE-120 form.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Resonses To Special Interrogatories Adn Requests For Production Of Documents; Supporting Memo P/A
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] The tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Petitioner Sharon Browne Nassiri's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories numbers 51, 52-54, 55, and 56-57 is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Petitioner's motion to compel further response to Request for Production (Set Three) number 75 is DENIED. The motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories 51 and 55 are denied as procedurally defective. Special Interrogatory 51 is not included in the Separate Statement, and Special Interrogatory number 55 is not listed in the Notice of Motion. The motion to compel further response to Special Interrogatories numbers 52, 53, 56 and 57 is denied on the merits. The motion to compel further response to Special Interrogatory number 54 - the source of the settlement funds - is granted. On a motion to compel brought under CCP section 2030.300, the burden is on the responding party to justify any objection or failure to fully answer the interrogatories. (Coy v. Sup. Ct of Contra Costa County (1962) 58 Cal. 2d 210, 220-221, and Fairmont Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 22 Cal. 4th 245, 255, citing Coy.) Respondent objects to each disputed Special Interrogatory on four grounds: (1) attorney-work product privilege, (2) compound question, (3) privacy interests in a confidential settlement agreement, and (4) on privacy rights under the California constitution, citing Hinshaw, et al. v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 51 Cal.App.4th 233, Doe 1 v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 1160, and Volkswagen of America, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1481. Respondent does not address the attorney-work product or compound objections in the Opposition papers. She has not met her burden to justify these objections. The Code of Civil Procedure sections Respondent cites in the Opposition also do not involve the type of settlement agreement at issue here and are inapplicable. Respondent's Opposition to the motion focuses on the privacy objections. When a privilege objection is asserted against a discovery request, the initial burden is on "the party asserting a privacy interest to establish its extent and the seriousness of the prospective invasion," and then the court must "weigh the countervailing interests the opposing party identifies." (Williams v. Sup.Ct. (2017) 3 Cal. 5th 531, 557.) The protection afforded by privacy rights is qualified, not absolute. The showing required to overcome the protection depends on the nature of the privacy right asserted. "The particular context, i.e., the specific kind of privacy interest involved and the nature and seriousness of the invasion and any countervailing interests, remains the critical factor in the analysis." (Hill v. National Collegiate Athletic Ass'n, 7 Cal.4th at 34.) The Hill elements are: (1) is there a legally protected privacy interest; (2) is there a reasonable expectation of privacy in the circumstances; and (3) is the conduct by [the requesting party] constituting a serious invasion of privacy, and a final balance of the privacy interest against "other interests." (7 Cal.4th at 34-40.) The party asserting privacy rights must carry the first three prongs, and then the court will engage in a "balancing" of any legitimate countervailing interests. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Resonses To Special Interrogatories Adn Requests For Production Of Documents (Line Added To Allow The Display Of Additional Text Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Respondent Rosie M. Thompson's evidentiary objections to parts of the declaration filed in support of Petitioner's Reply are overruled. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on April 20, 2021. Remote appearance is required, whenever possible, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Resonses To Special Interrogatories Adn Requests For Production Of Documents (Line Added To Allow The Display Of Additional Text Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] We turn first to the three Hill factors. Regarding factor one. a settlement agreement is entitled to "at least as much privacy protection as a bank account or tax information." (Hinshaw, Winkler, Draa, Marsh & Still (1996) 51 Cal. App. 4th 233, 241, disapproved of on other grounds by Williams v. Sup.Ct. (2017) 3 Cal. 5th 531.) Neither party disputes that this is a settlement agreement at issue in the disputed discovery. Regarding factor two, Respondent and Jane demonstrate they had an expectation of privacy in the settlement, as they agreed to a mutual non-disclosure clause in the settlement. Regarding factor three, absent a showing that the settlement is directly related to the Portside 503 Trust and the issues and claims in the pending Petition, Petitioner's demand for the terms of the settlement and a copy of the settlement document would constitute a serious invasion of the settling parties' privacy, as individuals. After the party asserting a privacy interest has made a showing on the three Hill factors, the court will engage in a "balancing" of any legitimate countervailing interests. The party seeking discovery must show a particularized need for the confidential information sought. The broad "relevant to the subject matter" standard under CCP section 2017.010 is not enough. There must be a showing that the information is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense. (Britt v. Sup.Ct. (San Diego Unified Port Dist.) (1978) 20 C3d 844, 859-862.) Here, Petitioner has made a particularized showing that the source of any settlement funds is directly relevant to the Petition's claim that the Portside 503 Trust still exists, and that Respondent is operating as its trustee, in breach of her fiduciary duties. Specifically, if the settlement was funded using trust resources, that would be directly relevant to Petitioner's claims. However, Petitioner has not made a sufficiently particularized showing of direct relevance as to the other Special Interrogatories. The interrogatories regarding the amount of money paid in connection with the settlement, who it was paid to or opinions about whether the settlement required court approval have not been sufficiently supported with a particularized showing at this stage in the litigation to warrant a compelled response. Respondent Rosie M. Thompson is ordered to produce further response to Special Interrogatory number 54 within 10 days of service of this order. Petitioner also moves under CCP section 2031.310(a) to compel further response to the document request. Respondent did not produce the settlement agreement and instead responded with the same privacy objections, and the one compound-question objection, as in the Special Interrogatories. A motion to compel under CCP section 2031.310 must set forth specific facts showing "good cause" justifying the discovery sought by the inspection demand. (CCP section 2031.310(b)(1).) That burden is met by a fact-specific showing of relevance unless there is a legitimate privilege issue or claim of attorney work product as there is here. (TBG Ins. Services Corp. v. Sup. Ct (2002) 96 Cal. App. 4th 443, 444.) When there is a legitimate privilege claim, the Hill factors are applied. Petitioner has not met her initial burden to make a factually specific showing of good cause justifying the discovery of the settlement agreement. Petitioner is seeking the document on the basis of conjecture about what it says. Petitioner does not make a showing that the settlement agreement itself is likely to have probative value related to the Petition's claims. Speculation about what is in the agreement does not constitute a factually specific showing of good cause. [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. status on close of estate set for 4/25/2022.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): ***the judicial officer is requesting that the parties be prepared to address Probate Code section 6124*** 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 3) The 04/01/2021 DE-121 does not show notice given to Michael Bassi who filed a Request for Special Notice on 01/28/2021. 4) Petitioner has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN ROBERT POBLOCKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN COLIN CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/11/2021. Notice must be given to the first and third named executors on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-121. Notice to the third named executor must be mailed to their place of business. Probate Code section 1215(a)(3).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN LA PEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine amount of bond. Petitioner is a nonresident and lives in Washington State. Petitioner requested minimum $20k bond and limited authority. Petition alleges $570,000.00 in assets all personal property.
